channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Justice Dept. drops 'New York Times' subpoenas under pressure from judge
item.description: The Justice Department is dropping the subpoenas it filed demanding grand jury testimony and phone records from New York Times journalists after sharp questioning Thursday from a federal judge.
item.content:encoded:
New York Times attorney David McGraw said after the government agreed to withdraw the subpoenas of Times journalists.'/>

The Justice Department is dropping the subpoenas it filed demanding grand jury testimony and phone records from New York Times journalists after sharp questioning Thursday from a federal judge.

(Image credit: NurPhoto via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Trump to impose double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries
item.description: The United States will slap taxes of 10% to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries accounting for 99% of U.S. imports, charging that they have inadequately enforced bans on goods produced by forced labor.
item.content:encoded:
The United States will slap taxes of 10% to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries accounting for 99% of U.S. imports, charging that they have inadequately enforced bans on goods produced by forced labor.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A town renamed its festival 'AI Love Irondale Day.' Then came the comments
item.description: Irondale, Alabama, rebranded its annual festival around AI, leading to online backlash that provoked a return to the original name.
item.content:encoded: Irondale, Alabama, rebranded its annual festival around AI, leading to online backlash that provoked a return to the original name.
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: A new threat in the Middle East sends oil over $100 a barrel again
item.description: The price of crude oil is rising again following Houthi claims of attacks on tankers in the Red Sea. What could it mean for gas prices, inflation and the global economy?
item.content:encoded: The price of crude oil is rising again following Houthi claims of attacks on tankers in the Red Sea. What could it mean for gas prices, inflation and the global economy?
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: 'New York Times' alleges White House abuses legal system to intimidate reporters
item.description: The New York Times will argue in court Thursday that the White House is abusing the justice system to intimidate reporters over a story that angered President Trump.
item.content:encoded:
The New York Times will argue in court Thursday that the White House is abusing the justice system to intimidate reporters over a story that angered President Trump. 

(Image credit: BRENDAN SMIALOWSKI/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: A town renamed its festival 'AI Love Irondale Day.' Then came the comments
item.description: The online outcry over the renaming of a summer festival illustrates the unease over the data center boom in the South.
item.content:encoded:
The online outcry over the renaming of a summer festival illustrates the unease over the data center boom in the South.

(Image credit: Stephan Bisaha)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: In South Dakota, public media endures a year after federal funding was wiped out
item.description: We check in on one state public broadcasting network a year after President Trump signed a law ending federal funding of public media.
item.content:encoded: We check in on one state public broadcasting network a year after President Trump signed a law ending federal funding of public media.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Staying in a job for the health insurance? About 1 in 4 Americans do, a survey says
item.description: About 24% of U.S. employees stay in unwanted jobs just for health insurance, a report out Wednesday finds. Up significantly since 2021, the uptick in "job lock" is a concerning sign for the U.S. economy.
item.content:encoded:
About 24% of U.S. employees stay in unwanted jobs just for health insurance, a report out Wednesday finds. Up significantly since 2021, the uptick in "job lock" is a concerning sign for the U.S. economy.

(Image credit: Creative Images Lab)
item.dc:creator: Joseph Kim

item.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on Canadian goods
item.description: President Donald Trump on Monday imposed 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, declaring that Canada has unfairly discriminated against American autos, alcohol and dairy products.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump on Monday imposed 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, declaring that Canada has unfairly discriminated against American autos, alcohol and dairy products.

(Image credit: Adrian Wyld)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The best economic ideas from around the world — Planet Money Summer School is back
item.description: Our free economics course for your ears is on a quest for great economics ideas.
item.content:encoded: Our free economics course for your ears is on a quest for great economics ideas.
item.dc:creator: Planet Money
